dimensions.note: Combined model + workflow-scope report that makes NO AWS calls and handles no credentials. The model reads already-stored upstream audit data via context.dataRepository; the report reads it from workflow stepExecutions. GlobalArgsSchema carries only upstream model ids/types (non-secret); nothing is vaulted, scoped, or sourced from the environment. The SchemaOf<T> doc-lint change touched no credential path.

Model coalesce logs one info at completion via a structured placeholder ('Coalesced {n} account(s) from {inst} instance + {role} role row(s)', aws_integration_coverage.ts:348). Report logs info on run start, an info collection summary, info on finish (with degraded flag), and a warn on the miswired-workflow path, all through tryLog which swallows a missing/throwing logger so logging never affects correctness. Structured placeholders throughout; no secrets are handled or logged. Unchanged by the SchemaOf annotation commit.

Model coalesce throws before any writeResource: it throws a descriptive 'run the audits first' error when both upstreams yielded zero usable rows, with a distinct 'No usable ... N upstream artifact(s) skipped' variant when rows existed but all failed decode/schema validation (both covered by tests). Per-artifact decode failures, storage-read rejections, and schema-mismatched rows are tolerantly skipped and counted (readSpec decodeSkipped + parseAll bad), never thrown. Report never throws: the entire collect+coalesce+render is wrapped, yielding degraded:true plus a stub markdown on any failure (covered by the exploding-context test). try/catch scopes are narrow (per-getContent read, per-log). Unchanged by the SchemaOf annotation commit.

Verified the full suite runs green (42 passed / 0 failed under `deno test --allow-read aws-integration-coverage/`). tests/coverage_test.ts pins the pure core (aggregateCoverage verdict matrix, refineMechanism this/other-stackset/standalone/manual/missing/unknown, aggregateMechanism mixed, representativeStacksetStatus precedence, reconcileAccount DISCREPANCY/stackset-behind, summarizeCoverage byRole rollup, end-to-end coalesce sort/derive). tests/aws_integration_coverage_test.ts drives coalesce through an in-memory fake repo: readSpec latest-version selection + deleted/null-bytes/read-failure skip, parseAll reject counting, the both-empty and all-invalid throw paths, the IAM-only matrix, and the write path (one coverage-<accountId> per account + exactly one summary, sources.skipped == decode+schema skips, dataHandles == writes, no AWS client). tests/integration_coverage_report_test.ts covers collect modelType filtering/spec routing/skip counting, renderMarkdown section presence, remediation-by-kind, the markdown+json-only no-csv contract, and the never-throws degraded path. The two colocated sibling tests pin both entrypoints to the manifest name/version, the two specs + single coalesce method, the upgrade-chain-ends-at-model.version invariant, and the report name/scope contract. No test relied on passthrough into a written row, so the schema-type narrowing did not weaken any assertion. (Note: the colocated tests use Deno.readTextFile on manifest.yaml, so a no-permission `deno test` run reports them as 2 NotCapable failures — a sandbox permission artifact, not a code defect; they pass with --allow-read.)

Pure read-and-derive over already-stored upstream data; makes no external mutation. coverage rows are keyed coverage-<accountId> and the summary is the fixed 'summary' key, so re-runs overwrite the same instances deterministically (rows sorted by accountId, roleNames sorted, byRole sorted for stable output). Resilient to partial/corrupt upstream artifacts (skipped + counted) and to a degraded upstream sweep; the report adds the degraded:true outer guard. No created cloud resources to orphan. Unchanged by the SchemaOf annotation commit.

Makes NO external HTTP/AWS calls. Its only contracts are the upstream resource schemas it consumes (StackSet instance/summary rows, IAM role/scan_error rows), re-validated per row with zod safeParse before use — the model via dataRepository.findAllForModel/getContent, the report via the workflow stepExecutions data handles. No pagination, retries, or rate limits to honor.

Creates no SDK clients, network connections, file handles, or temp files. Reads stored bytes (TextDecoder over getContent) and returns model writes / markdown + JSON; there is nothing to clean up or track on any code path.

Grepped the whole package dir (rg --hidden): zero 12-digit account-id literals, zero IPv4 literals, no .internal/.local/.corp hostnames, no arn:/vpc-/subnet- identifiers. README and the JSON example use clearly-fictional placeholders (acme-coverage workflow, acme-readonly stackset, AcmeReadonly role) and explicitly label account ids as illustrative; test fixtures use ACCT_ALPHA/BETA/GAMMA labels and the acme-ss stackset; 'us-east-1' is a public AWS region name, not an identifier. The NEW exported SchemaOf<T> interface (_lib/coverage.ts:30) is a generic public type seam carrying only a safeParse signature — it exposes no infrastructure data and its JSDoc references only the generic zod type names z.ZodObject/z.ZodType, not real values.

The model's OWN written, CEL-queryable resource schemas are plain z.object with NO .passthrough(): CoverageRowSchema (_lib/coverage.ts:301-321, 13 fields) backs the per-account 'coverage' resource and SummaryResourceSchema (aws_integration_coverage.ts:72-102, 17 fields incl. the nested 5-key sources object) backs the 'summary' resource — both verified field-for-field against the objects passed to writeResource and matching 1:1, so CEL validation sees the exact resource shape. The SchemaOf<T> annotation commit (9c92dd3) only narrowed the STATIC type of the exported schemas; the runtime zod objects are byte-identical (CoverageRowSchema is still z.object, the upstream decoders still .passthrough()). The four MIRRORED UPSTREAM decoders (InstanceSchema/SummarySchema/RoleSchema/IamErrorSchema, _lib:61-104) intentionally retain .passthrough() + safeParse: they tolerantly decode another producer's output, are NOT this model's own resources, and are never written, so passthrough there is correct by design — consistent with the prior 2026.06.13.0 review. RoleDetailSchema is now annotated SchemaOf<unknown> and is no longer referenced inside CoverageRowSchema (a non-exported roleDetailObject is used there instead); it is an exported public symbol only, not in any write path, so the swap is benign.

Both model specs declare lifetime: 'infinite' and garbageCollection: 10 (coverage at aws_integration_coverage.ts:256-257, summary at :264-265). The report persists nothing of its own (the host stores report-<name> / report-<name>-json), so GC config is not applicable to the report half. Unchanged by the SchemaOf annotation commit.

Read-only coalesce model, not a CRUD lifecycle. coalesce re-derives the per-account coverage matrix + rollup summary each run from upstream data; this model owns no resource to create/update/delete, and re-running coalesce is the refresh mechanism, so a separate sync method is unnecessary.
coalesce makes no AWS calls and performs no mutation — it only reads stored data and writes derived swamp rows. There is no destructive action a policy/live pre-flight label would gate.

Mechanical check: the coverage spec is keyed coverage-<accountId> (unique per account) and the summary spec is the singleton key 'summary'. Distinct specs, distinct key prefixes, single writer (coalesce) per spec — no cross-spec or cross-method collision possible. Schema-write conformance holds: coverage writes are CoverageRow objects matching CoverageRowSchema 1:1; the summary object's 17 fields match SummaryResourceSchema 1:1 with no placeholder fields (each computed from rollup/result or echoed from the source ids).

The model legitimately reads OTHER models' stored data via dataRepository.findAllForModel + getContent (a cross-model coalesce of the stackset-audit and iam-role-audit instances). The readResource-vs-dataRepository guidance concerns reading back a model's OWN state in update/delete/sync; this model never reads back its own coverage/summary rows — it reads upstream producers' data, for which dataRepository is the correct API. readSpec keeps the latest active version per data name and skips deleted/undecodable artifacts. Not an issue.

The version bump to 2026.06.16.0 DOES add a matching upgrades entry: model.upgrades (aws_integration_coverage.ts:236-248) now carries two identity entries, the second with toVersion '2026.06.16.0', description 'Doc-lint release (explicit schema type annotations); no schema changes', and upgradeAttributes (old) => old. Identity migration is correct because the SchemaOf<T> change is a pure static-type narrowing — the stored attribute shape is unchanged, so no data migration is needed. The colocated test asserts upgrades.at(-1).toVersion === model.version, which guards this invariant. Satisfies the reference's 'a version bump should add an upgrades entry'.
